title: Debugging, Review, and Adversarial Prompting · Failure Case: Reproduction
summary: Diagnose a failing build and review the repair against a base branch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt reproduction increment in an evidence-first debug and review playbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.
Require command output, behavior checks, and a concise unresolved-risk handoff.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Reproduction evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-reproduction-n6fjfi" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Reproduction into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-reproduction-n6fjfi
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Reproduction model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Reproduction applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.
